engineGuide.h2: Jeep Wagoneer Engine Codes - Full Technical Specifications & Replacement Guide
engineGuide.h3: Every engine code fitted to the Jeep Wagoneer across all generations, with technical specs, compatible variants and trims, known failure points, and average rebuilt prices from UK specialists. Select any engine code to compare quotes.

entries.title: 5.7L Petrol
entries.history: Dominant Wagoneer powertrain with eTorque 48V mild-hybrid assist for refined stop-start and torque fill.
compatibleVariants: Series I, Series II, Series III, Wagoneer L, 4x4, 4x2, Tow Package
commonFailures: Lifter collapse and camshaft wear documented in TSB 2022-089, typically 40k-70k miles
eTorque belt-driven starter-generator electrical faults causing derate mode
Exhaust manifold bolt failure leading to ticking noise and emissions warnings

commonFailures: eTorque 48V battery degradation affecting hybrid assist function
BSG motor calibration issues requiring software update
Same lifter/cam vulnerability as EZH if not addressed under TSB
entries.cta: Get quotes for EZL Jeep Wagoneer engine replacement

Intercooler condensation management in UK climate conditions

commonProblems.h2: Jeep Wagoneer Engine Problems - What It Costs to Fix & When Replacement Makes Sense
commonProblems.h3: The modern WS-platform Jeep Wagoneer (2022-present) features advanced Hemi, Hurricane and Pentastar engines, with the 5.7L Hemi eTorque dominating UK grey-import replacement enquiries. Every rebuilt engine supplied through EngineMarket includes upgraded internal components designed to address original failure points - plus a minimum 12‑month unlimited mileage warranty.

problems.h4: Jeep Wagoneer Hemi Lifter Failure - Why 5.7L Engines Fail Prematurely
problems.affectedModels: Jeep Wagoneer Series I/II/III, Wagoneer L (2022-2024, EZH/EZL 5.7L Hemi eTorque)
problems.rootCause: TSB 2022-089 documents hydraulic lifter collapse causing camshaft lobe wear, often triggered by oil aeration in eTorque systems.

repairOptions.tier: Minor - Preventative Lifter Inspection - Early diagnosis before cam damage
repairOptions.whatItInvolves: Valve cover removal, lifter bore inspection, oil system flush
repairOptions.longevity: Viable only if caught before cam scoring; not a permanent fix

repairOptions.tier: Intermediate - Lifter & Cam Replacement - Addressing documented wear
repairOptions.whatItInvolves: Full valvetrain teardown, upgraded lifters, camshaft, timing components
repairOptions.longevity: Extends life but doesn't address block wear; risk of recurrence

repairOptions.tier: Full Replacement - Rebuilt / Reconditioned Engine - Complete solution
repairOptions.whatItInvolves: Supply & fit rebuilt unit with upgraded valvetrain, bench-tested, 12-month warranty
repairOptions.longevity: Most reliable long-term solution for £60k-£90k vehicle

problems.recommendation: On a Wagoneer worth £60k+, preventative inspection at 40k miles is wise, but once lifter tick is audible, cam damage is likely. For grey-import owners, a rebuilt EZH/EZL with TSB-compliant components offers the best value and peace of mind.
problems.cta: Compare rebuilt EZH engine prices for your Jeep Wagoneer

problems.h4: Jeep Wagoneer eTorque BSG Failure - 48V Hybrid System Faults
problems.affectedModels: Jeep Wagoneer Series I/II/III with eTorque (2022-2024, EZH/EZL)
problems.rootCause: Belt-driven starter-generator electrical faults, 48V battery degradation, or control module software issues triggering derate mode.

repairOptions.tier: Minor - BSG Diagnostic & Software Update - Addressing calibration issues
repairOptions.whatItInvolves: OBD scan, BSG motor test, ECU flash update, belt tension check

repairOptions.tier: Intermediate - BSG Motor Replacement - Hardware component swap
repairOptions.whatItInvolves: BSG unit replacement, 48V battery health check, system recalibration
repairOptions.longevity: Resolves motor faults but doesn't address underlying electrical wear

repairOptions.tier: Full Replacement - Rebuilt eTorque Engine - Integrated solution
repairOptions.whatItInvolves: Supply & fit rebuilt EZL unit with calibrated BSG, 48V system validation, warranty
repairOptions.longevity: Only option guaranteeing hybrid functionality on high-value grey-import

problems.recommendation: eTorque systems require specialist 48V expertise. If derate mode persists after software update, the BSG motor is likely failing. For UK grey-import compliance, a rebuilt engine with calibrated hybrid components is the safest investment.
problems.cta: Get quotes for EZL eTorque engine replacement UK

problems.h4: Jeep Wagoneer Hurricane Turbo Failure - Early-Production Twin-Turbo Issues
problems.affectedModels: Jeep Wagoneer Series III, Grand Wagoneer (2024-present, GME-T6)
problems.rootCause: Turbo actuator linkage wear, wastegate rattle from manufacturing tolerance issues, or software calibration mismatches affecting boost control.

repairOptions.tier: Minor - Turbo Actuator Calibration - Software-focused fix
repairOptions.whatItInvolves: Actuator motor test, linkage adjustment, ECU boost map update
repairOptions.longevity: Only viable for calibration-related boost issues

repairOptions.tier: Intermediate - Turbocharger Replacement - Hardware component swap
repairOptions.whatItInvolves: Single turbo replacement, intercooler inspection, system relearn
repairOptions.longevity: Addresses one turbo but doesn't prevent twin failure

repairOptions.tier: Full Replacement - Rebuilt Hurricane Engine - Complete powertrain solution
repairOptions.whatItInvolves: Supply & fit rebuilt GME-T6 with upgraded turbo components, dyno validation, warranty
repairOptions.longevity: Only solution guaranteeing twin-turbo reliability on cutting-edge powertrain

problems.recommendation: Hurricane engines are new to UK grey-imports. If wastegate rattle or boost loss occurs, specialist diagnostic is essential. Given the £85k+ vehicle value, a rebuilt GME-T6 with upgraded turbo components offers the most secure long-term solution.
problems.cta: Enquire about GME-T6 Hurricane engine specialist rebuild

problems.group: Jeep Wagoneer Exhaust Manifold Bolt Failure
problems.h4: Jeep Wagoneer Exhaust Manifold Bolt Failure - Hemi Ticking Noise
problems.affectedModels: Jeep Wagoneer all trims with Hemi V8 (2022-2024, EZH/EZL/ESF)
problems.rootCause: Thermal cycling stress on exhaust manifold studs causing bolt fracture, leading to exhaust leak, ticking noise, and MoT emissions failure risk.

repairOptions.tier: Minor - Manifold Stud Replacement - Addressing broken bolts only
repairOptions.whatItInvolves: Manifold removal, stud extraction/replacement, gasket renewal
repairOptions.longevity: Temporary fix; adjacent studs often fail subsequently

repairOptions.tier: Intermediate - Manifold Assembly Replacement - Full component swap
repairOptions.whatItInvolves: Complete manifold assembly replacement with upgraded hardware
repairOptions.longevity: Better longevity but doesn't address underlying thermal stress design

repairOptions.tier: Full Replacement - Rebuilt Engine with Upgraded Manifold - Integrated solution
repairOptions.whatItInvolves: Supply & fit rebuilt unit with reinforced manifold hardware, emissions-tested, warranty
repairOptions.longevity: Only solution ensuring MoT compliance and long-term reliability on grey-import

problems.recommendation: Exhaust leaks trigger MoT failures in the UK. While stud replacement is cheaper short-term, the thermal stress issue often recurs. For grey-import Wagoneers requiring UK road legality, a rebuilt engine with upgraded manifold hardware is the most compliant, durable solution.
problems.cta: Compare Hemi engine replacement prices with MoT-compliant rebuild

problems.group: Jeep Wagoneer Pentastar Timing Chain Failure
problems.h4: Jeep Wagoneer Pentastar Timing Chain Failure - Base Model Vulnerability
problems.rootCause: Timing chain tensioner wear allowing chain slack, causing rattle on cold start and potential valve timing disruption.

repairOptions.tier: Minor - Tensioner & Guide Replacement - Preventative maintenance
repairOptions.whatItInvolves: Front cover removal, tensioner/guide swap, timing reset
repairOptions.longevity: Effective if caught before chain stretch causes valve interference

repairOptions.tier: Intermediate - Full Timing Component Replacement - Comprehensive repair
repairOptions.whatItInvolves: Chain, tensioners, guides, phasers replaced; full timing system overhaul
repairOptions.longevity: Addresses all wear points but labour-intensive on transverse V6

repairOptions.tier: Full Replacement - Rebuilt Pentastar Engine - Complete solution
repairOptions.whatItInvolves: Supply & fit rebuilt ERC/ERF with upgraded timing components, bench-tested, warranty
repairOptions.longevity: Most reliable option for base-model Wagoneer owners prioritising longevity

problems.recommendation: Pentastar timing issues are well-documented. If cold-start rattle is present, tensioner failure is likely advanced. For cost-conscious Wagoneer owners, a rebuilt ERC/ERF with upgraded timing components offers better value than repeated repairs on a £50k+ vehicle.
problems.cta: Get quotes for ERC Pentastar engine replacement UK

finalCta.h4: Don't Let Engine Failure Write Off Your Jeep Wagoneer
finalCta.paragraph: The modern Wagoneer represents a £60k-£90k investment. When engine issues arise, comparing quotes from EngineMarket's vetted UK specialists ensures you get a rebuilt unit with upgraded components addressing the original failure point - not just a like-for-like replacement. Every engine includes a minimum 12‑month unlimited mileage warranty, nationwide delivery, and grey-import compliance documentation for UK road legality.
finalCta.buttonText: Compare Jeep Wagoneer Engine Replacement Prices Now →
finalCta.disclaimer: All prices are indicative UK market ranges based on historical quote data and repair requests from our network of engine specialists. Dealer pricing is estimated based on published main dealer labour rates and genuine OEM parts pricing. Specialist pricing reflects actual quote data from EngineMarket's UK network. Actual costs vary depending on damage severity, vehicle condition, variant, and workshop location within the UK. Use our free comparison tool to get tailored quotes for your specific Jeep Wagoneer and engine code. Every engine supplied through EngineMarket carries a minimum 12‑month unlimited mileage warranty - always confirm exact terms with your chosen supplier.

engineYears.h2: Jeep Wagoneer Engines by Year - What Was Fitted & When
engineYears.intro: This technical timeline covers all major engine changes across the modern Jeep Wagoneer (2022-present), organised so UK grey-import owners can quickly identify their engine and compare rebuilt prices from specialists.

years.preview: WS Platform Launch: Hemi eTorque & Pentastar Debut
years.description: The modern Wagoneer launched with 5.7L Hemi eTorque as the dominant powertrain, plus base-model 3.6L Pentastar for cost-conscious grey-import buyers.
keyChanges: WS-platform Wagoneer introduced to UK grey-import market
5.7L Hemi EZH with eTorque 48V mild-hybrid as volume seller
3.6L Pentastar ERC offered on base trims for lower entry price
Grand Wagoneer launched with exclusive 6.4L Hemi ESF
mainEngines: EZH - 5.7L petrol with eTorque MHEV (Series I/II/III, Wagoneer L)
knownFor: Strong initial grey-import demand for Hemi eTorque models
Early reports of lifter tick prompting TSB 2022-089 investigation
eTorque system complexity requiring specialist UK calibration expertise
Grand Wagoneer ESF commanding premium rebuild pricing for luxury buyers
years.cta: Get quotes for 2022 Jeep Wagoneer replacement engines
years.ticker: Jeep Wagoneer Hemi lifter failure • Wagoneer eTorque BSG fault • Grand Wagoneer exhaust manifold bolt

years.preview: Mid-Cycle Refinements: EZL Calibration & Wagoneer L Launch
years.description: Late-production EZH variants received eTorque calibration refinements (EZL code), while the extended-wheelbase Wagoneer L expanded capability-focused grey-import options.
keyChanges: EZL engine code introduced with refined eTorque calibration for improved reliability
Wagoneer L long-wheelbase variant launched, requiring calibrated replacements for added weight
Software updates addressing early Hemi lifter and eTorque BSG concerns
Increased UK grey-import volume driving specialist rebuild demand
mainEngines: EZL - 5.7L petrol with refined eTorque calibration (late-production)
EZH - 5.7L petrol with eTorque (continued volume production)
ERF - 3.6L petrol Pentastar with software refinements (base trim)
knownFor: EZL code offering marginal reliability improvements over early EZH
Wagoneer L requiring calibrated replacements for extended chassis weight distribution
Growing UK specialist network for eTorque 48V system expertise
Grey-import compliance documentation becoming critical for MoT registration
years.cta: Get quotes for 2023 Jeep Wagoneer replacement engines
years.ticker: Wagoneer EZL eTorque battery • Wagoneer L engine calibration • Grey-import MoT compliance

years.preview: Hurricane Twin-Turbo Introduction & Series III Refresh
years.description: The new GME-T6 Hurricane twin-turbo inline-six debuted in Series III and Grand Wagoneer trims, offering superior efficiency but introducing early-production turbo actuator concerns for UK specialists.
keyChanges: GME-T6 Hurricane 3.0L twin-turbo I6 introduced replacing Hemi in top trims
Series III refresh with updated software addressing prior Hemi/eTorque concerns
Grand Wagoneer offering both ESF 6.4L and Hurricane High Output options
Increased focus on UK grey-import compliance documentation for new engine codes
mainEngines: GME-T6 - 3.0L petrol twin-turbo Hurricane (Series III, Grand Wagoneer)
EZL - 5.7L petrol with eTorque (continued on mid-trims)
knownFor: Hurricane delivering superior torque and efficiency vs. Hemi
Early-production turbo actuator and wastegate rattle concerns requiring specialist attention
Software calibration critical for UK emissions compliance on new GME-T6 code
Premium pricing for Hurricane rebuilds reflecting cutting-edge twin-turbo complexity
years.cta: Enquire about 2024 Jeep Wagoneer Hurricane engine availability
years.ticker: Hurricane turbo actuator failure • GME-T6 software calibration • Series III engine warranty

faq.h2: Jeep Wagoneer Engine Replacement - Frequently Asked Questions

items.question: How much does a Jeep Wagoneer engine replacement cost in the UK?
items.answer: Typical rebuilt Jeep Wagoneer engine prices range from £4,800 for base Pentastar units to £9,200 for Grand Wagoneer Hemi, with supply & fit adding £1,800-£3,200 depending on variant and specialist location.
keyPoints: EZH/EZL 5.7L Hemi eTorque rebuilt: £5,250-£7,250 supply only
GME-T6 Hurricane twin-turbo rebuilt: £6,500-£8,750 supply only
Grey-import compliance documentation included in specialist quotes
items.cta: Compare Jeep Wagoneer engine replacement prices UK

items.question: What engine codes fit the modern Jeep Wagoneer?
items.answer: The WS-platform Wagoneer (2022-present) uses EZH/EZL 5.7L Hemi eTorque, GME-T6 3.0L Hurricane, ERC/ERF 3.6L Pentastar, and ESF 6.4L Hemi for Grand Wagoneer.
keyPoints: EZH/EZL dominate UK grey-import replacement enquiries
GME-T6 requires specialist twin-turbo calibration expertise
Verify your code via VIN decoder before requesting quotes
items.cta: Find your Jeep Wagoneer engine code via VIN lookup

items.question: Is the Jeep Wagoneer Hemi engine reliable?
items.answer: The 5.7L Hemi eTorque is powerful but has documented lifter/camshaft vulnerabilities (TSB 2022-089); rebuilt units with upgraded valvetrain components offer the most reliable long-term solution for grey-import owners.
keyPoints: TSB 2022-089 addresses lifter collapse from 40k-70k miles
eTorque 48V system requires specialist hybrid calibration expertise
Rebuilt EZH/EZL with TSB-compliant parts recommended for £60k+ vehicles
items.cta: Get quotes for TSB-compliant Hemi engine rebuild UK

items.question: How long does a Jeep Wagoneer engine replacement take?
items.answer: Supply-only rebuilt engines typically dispatch within 3-5 working days from UK specialists; full supply & fit service including calibration and grey-import compliance documentation takes 5-7 working days.
keyPoints: eTorque and Hurricane variants require additional calibration time
Grey-import compliance documentation prepared concurrently with engine build
Nationwide delivery available; collection from specialist workshop optional
items.cta: Book Jeep Wagoneer engine supply & fit appointment UK

items.question: What warranty comes with a rebuilt Jeep Wagoneer engine?
items.answer: All rebuilt Jeep Wagoneer engines supplied through EngineMarket include a minimum 12-month unlimited mileage warranty, with extended 18-24 month options available for Grand Wagoneer and Hurricane variants.
keyPoints: Warranty covers valvetrain components for Hemi codes (EZH/EZL/ESF)
Turbo components covered for Hurricane GME-T6 rebuilds
eTorque 48V system calibration included in hybrid variant warranty
items.cta: Compare Jeep Wagoneer engine warranty options UK

items.question: Can I replace my Wagoneer engine myself?
items.answer: Due to complex eTorque 48V integration, Hurricane turbo calibration, and Quadra-Drive II 4x4 compatibility, professional specialist fitting is strongly recommended for UK grey-import Wagoneers to preserve performance and compliance.
keyPoints: eTorque BSG motor requires 48V system diagnostic equipment
Hurricane turbo mapping needs specialist ECU calibration tools
MoT emissions testing requires validated post-install calibration
items.cta: Find certified Jeep Wagoneer engine fitting specialist UK

items.question: Are used Jeep Wagoneer engines a good option?
items.answer: Used Wagoneer engines offer budget savings but require thorough verification of grey-import history, service records, and compression testing to avoid inheriting undocumented lifter or eTorque issues common on early WS-platform units.
keyPoints: Request full DVLA and US title history for grey-import donor vehicles
Compression test and borescope inspection essential pre-purchase
Typical used engine warranty: 3-6 months parts only vs. 12-month rebuilt
items.cta: Check used Jeep Wagoneer engine availability UK

items.question: Will a replacement engine affect my Wagoneer's 4x4 system?
items.answer: All rebuilt Jeep Wagoneer engines supplied through EngineMarket maintain factory 4x4 calibration, preserving Quadra-Drive II torque mapping and low-range specifications for towing and off-road capability.
keyPoints: ECU relearn ensures 4WD system compatibility post-installation
Tow package variants receive enhanced cooling system validation
Off-road calibration preserved for trail-rated Wagoneer variants
items.cta: Compare 4x4-compatible Jeep Wagoneer engine prices UK

items.question: How do I ensure my grey-import Wagoneer engine is UK compliant?
items.answer: EngineMarket specialists provide MoT emissions certification, IVA support documentation, and ULEZ compliance verification for all rebuilt Wagoneer engines, ensuring legal UK road use post-replacement.
keyPoints: Exhaust emissions testing included for Hemi and Hurricane variants
eTorque 48V system calibration validated for UK MoT standards
Documentation package supports grey-import registration and insurance
items.cta: Get grey-import compliant Jeep Wagoneer engine quote UK

items.question: Is it worth replacing a Jeep Wagoneer engine vs. selling?
items.answer: With Wagoneer values ranging £60k-£90k, a rebuilt engine (£4,800-£9,200) typically preserves £40k+ in vehicle equity versus selling with a faulty engine, making replacement the financially prudent choice for grey-import owners.
keyPoints: Rebuilt engine cost represents 8-15% of typical Wagoneer market value
TSB-compliant rebuilds enhance resale value and buyer confidence
Supply & fit service minimises downtime for high-value grey-import vehicles
items.cta: Calculate Jeep Wagoneer engine replacement ROI UK
